celebration: GRADUATION — the whole cast graduates and the album completes

songs.title: Z-Z-Zip
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
zzz… zzz… zzz…
(a buzzing bee, motor on)

[Hook 1 — kids chant]
Z! Z! Zzz-zzz-zzz!
What begins with Z?
Z-z-zebra! Z-z-zebra!
It's a zebra! (a zebra!)

[Hook 2 — kids chant]
Z! Z! Zzz-zzz-zzz!
What begins with Z?
Z-z-zip! Z-z-zip!
It's a zip! (a zip!)

[Hook 3 — kids chant]
Z! Z! Zzz-zzz-zzz!
What begins with Z?
Z-z-zero! Z-z-zero!
It's a zero! (a zero!)

[Breakdown — whispered, bass drops out]
zzz… (motor on) … sss… (off)
zip… sip… zap… sap…
buzz… fizz… (double z, big buzz)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (last time now… NO!)
P-p-potato! Not Z!
(zzz… zzz… zzz…)

[Final Hook — big]
Z! Z! Zzz-zzz-zzz!
Zigzag! Zigzag! BUZZ!
The bug can buzz, buzz, buzz!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The last sound of the year. Z is a VOICED buzz /z/ like a bee — motor ON, contrast with /s/ motor OFF (zip vs sip). NEVER 'zed/zee' (letter name) and NEVER 'zuh' (schwa). Double-z (buzz, fizz) is one long buzz. Land the final hook on 'the bug can buzz' — the graduation book's title.

songs.title: The Bug Can Buzz
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — the whole year comes back]
(It's a… where is… on the mat…)

[Hook]
The bug can buzz! The bug can buzz!
What can the bug do? (buzz! buzz!)
The bug can buzz!

[Verse 2 — the cast graduates]
The cat can nap. The dog can dig.
The rat can run. The duck can quack.
The fox can mix. The ant can tag.
We can read! (we can read!)

[Verse 3 — the year's frames]
It's a potato! (It's a ___!)
Where is Sejeena? (on the mat!)
Zip! Zap! Zigzag!
The bug can BUZZ!

[Bridge]
Sam can. Sejeena can.
The cat is on the mat.
We can read it — every one!
(we did it! we did it!)

[Outro — the album completes]
And the potato can…?!
(POTATO!)
The bug can buzz…
The end. (the end!)
(buzz… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: 🎓 THE GRADUATION ANTHEM — the finale of the whole album. Core frame 'The ___ can ___!' but this one runs a little longer (~20 unique words) because it quotes back the YEAR: 'It's a ___' (W1), 'Where is Segina?' (W2), 'on the mat' (W4), each cast member's verb. Let the choir swell on 'we can read!'. End on the potato, then a soft 'the end'. The 'we can read' line is sung, not printed — the printed book stays strictly decodable.

spreads.artNote: The Bug (red-shelled cast beetle) center stage in a tiny graduation cap, wings just starting to lift. The whole year has led here.
The Bug airborne, wings a golden blur, a visible 'BUZZ' shimmer around it, joyful flight.
The Bug zipping across the spread in a streak of motion lines, leaving a zigzag trail. Speed.
The Cat (tabby) curled asleep on the familiar mat — a Week 4 callback, the very first sentence, honored.
The Dog (brown) mid-dig, dirt flying, tail up, doing what a Week 12 dog does best.
The Rat (grey) sprinting joyfully, little legs a blur — the Week 16 verb, the Week 15 friend.
The Duck (white) quacking proudly in a graduation cap, sock finally set down — a Week 25 callback.
The Fox (red) stirring a big bowl, tail wrapped round a spoon — the Week 24 verb, the sly one being useful for once.
The Ant (black) tagging the Bug in a game, both tiny and delighted — the Week 6 friend, first cast member back.
A big celebratory Z-spread — lightning zaps, zigzag streamers, the Bug buzzing loops overhead. The letter's grand finale.

spreads.text: Sam can. Segina can. The bug can buzz!
spreads.artNote: GRADUATION: the whole cast in tiny caps on the mat — Cat, Ant, Dog, Rat, Bug, Duck, Fox, and Sam and Segina beaming, an open book in the middle they can all read. The family, complete.

THE FINALE: the potato in a graduation cap and round glasses at the little whiteboard, pointer up, taking one last bow. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS the word from the picture. The album completes. Curtain.

extras: Motor on / motor off: /z/ buzz (zip) vs /s/ hiss (sip) — hand on the throat, feel the bee wake up
Double-z: buzz and fizz end in TWO z's said as one long buzz — the FLSZ doubling rule they meet next level
🎓 GRADUATION CEREMONY: every child reads a decodable book aloud to the whole cast, gets a paper cap, and takes home the completed album — 26 weeks, 26 sounds, the whole alphabet decoded
The completed album: play all 51 songs end to end while the caps go on

teacherFocus: The last sound of the year. Z is the voiced twin of /s/ — motor on (zip vs sip), hand on the throat feels the bee wake up. Double-z (buzz, fizz) is one long buzz. Then GRADUATE: every child reads a decodable book aloud to the whole cast, earns a paper cap, and takes home the completed 26-week album. The book quotes the whole year back — read the graduation spread slowly and name every friend and every sound they conquered.
